task.question: Find a recent TypeScript or JavaScript library release after August 2025 that introduced at least three concrete API changes and identify source-backed examples.
task.agentGoal: This mirrors Exa WebCode-style coding-agent search. Prefer changelogs, release notes, and official docs.

callRationale: Used tavily_search with start_date=2025-09-01 and time_range=year to enforce the task's post-August-2025 freshness requirement.
Started with a broad official-source search across react.dev and github.com, then narrowed to react.dev after the first result set was too GitHub-heavy and noisy.
Used tavily_extract on the React 19.2 release post and three official API reference pages to ground the final answer in primary sources and concrete examples.
strengths: Official React sources dominate the evidence.
The selected release is explicitly dated October 1, 2025 and therefore satisfies the freshness constraint.
The final answer identifies three concrete APIs and includes examples or signatures from official docs.
weaknesses: The first broad search returned several irrelevant GitHub results before narrowing to the official React domain.
Tavily extract returned compact, query-reranked excerpts, so some longer examples were partially summarized rather than fully reproduced.

notableFailures.category: direct fix and needs no further changes
notableFailures.description: The first args JSON was accidentally created relative to the chat workspace instead of /Users/ethanaggor/firecrawl-results. I corrected by writing the args file under evals/work/real-20260425-needle-technical-release-tavily/ and trashed the misplaced untracked JSON file.

notableFailures.category: direct fix and needs no further changes
notableFailures.description: The first Tavily search was too broad and returned noisy GitHub results. I fixed this by issuing a freshness-bounded search restricted to react.dev.
